summary: The CI job failed due to a compile error in the SIMD header file `pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp` at line 255, which caused the meson build to fail and package metadata generation to abort. The error was triggered during the build of pandas C++ extensions on macOS 15 (ARM64).
root_cause: A C++ compile error in `pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp` at line 255, column 50 caused the meson-based build to fail, resulting in metadata-generation-failed and a non-zero exit code.

evidence.reason: This is the compiler error location — a C++ compile error at a specific line and column in the SIMD header file, indicating the root cause is a compile failure in that file.
The compiler reported 2 errors, confirming the build did not succeed due to compile-time failures.
The pip/build frontend could not generate package metadata because the underlying meson build subprocess failed due to the compile error.
The build subprocess exited with an error, confirming the compile failure propagated up to the packaging layer.
Meson was invoked as the build backend, and its failure (due to the compile error) caused the overall metadata generation to fail.
The GitHub Actions step exited with code 1, confirming the job failed.

suggested_fix: Investigate and fix the C++ compile error in `pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp` at line 255, column 50 — likely an incompatibility with the NEON SIMD intrinsics on macOS 15 / ARM64. Check recent changes to that file and verify the xsimd/NEON64 code path compiles correctly on Apple Silicon.
